The PR #57821 added OpenTelemetry trace support for `code_interpreter_call`, `file_search_call`, `web_search_call`, `mcp_call`, and other tool types in `OpenTelemetryResponseScope.cs`, along with new test files. The documentation gaps are:

2026-04-07T21:47:47.7938982Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T21:47:35.486434846Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/`\n**Triggered by:** PR #57821 (commit 6990ac18d) by @M-Hietala\n\n## What Changed\n\nPR #57821 expanded `OpenTelemetryResponseScope.cs` to emit trace spans and attributes for additional tool call types used in the OpenAI Responses API:\n\n- `code_interpreter_call` — captures `code`, `outputs`, `status`\n- `file_search_call` — captures `queries`, `results`, `status`\n- `web_search_call` — captures `action`, `status`\n- `mcp_call` / `mcp_*` variants — captures `name`, `arguments`, `server_label`, `status`\n\nNew telemetry test files were also added: `ResponsesTelemetryTests.CodeInterpreter.cs`, `ResponsesTelemetryTests.FileSearch.cs`, `ResponsesTelemetryTests.Mcp.cs`, and `ResponsesTelemetryTests.WebSearch.cs`.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **CHANGELOG** (`CHANGELOG.md`): The `## 2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` \"Features Added\" section is empty. The expanded tool call tracing should be listed here.\n- **README tracing section** (`README.md`): The existing \"Tracing\" section covers setup (enabling GenAI tracing, content recording, Azure Monitor / console exporters) but does not document *which tool types* are instrumented or *what span attributes* are captured per type. Users have no guidance on what trace data they can expect when using Code Interpreter, File Search, Web Search, or MCP tools.\n- **No sample demonstrating tool tracing**: `tests/Samples/Sample_Responses_Tracing.cs` only shows a simple text-prompt trace. There is no snippet showing how tool-call spans appear in traces, which the README could link to.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n#### File 1: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/CHANGELOG.md`\n\nEdit the `## 2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section:\n\n```markdown\n## 2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Expanded OpenTelemetry tracing support to additional tool call types in the Responses API:\n  - `code_interpreter_call` — traces code execution with `code`, `outputs`, and `status` fields (when content recording is enabled)\n  - `file_search_call` — traces file search operations with `queries`, `results`, and `status` fields (when content recording is enabled)\n  - `web_search_call` — traces web search calls with `action` and `status` fields (when content recording is enabled)\n  - `mcp_call` and `mcp_*` variants — traces MCP tool calls with `name`, `arguments`, `server_label`, and `status` fields (when content recording is enabled)\n  - Generic fallback tracing for any unrecognized `*_call` output item types\n```\n\n#### File 2: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/README.md`\n\nAdd a new subsection **\"Traced tool types\"** inside the existing `### Tracing` section, after the \"Enabling content recording\" subsection and before `## Troubleshooting`. Insert:\n\n```markdown\n### Traced tool types\n\nWhen GenAI tracing is enabled, the SDK emits a `gen_ai.tool.message` event on the response span for each tool call present in the response. The following tool types are supported:\n\n| Tool type | Always captured | Captured when content recording is enabled |\n|---|---|---|\n| `function_call` | call ID | function name, arguments |\n| `code_interpreter_call` | call ID, status | code, outputs |\n| `file_search_call` | call ID, status | queries, results |\n| `web_search_call` | call ID, status | action |\n| `mcp_call` | call ID, server label, status | name, arguments |\n| `mcp_*` (other MCP variants) | call ID, server label, status | name, arguments, input, output |\n\n\u003e **Note:** Binary data such as images or generated files is never captured in traces regardless of content recording settings.\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nNo new README snippet is strictly required for the CHANGELOG fix. However, optionally add a snippet demonstrating a response that triggers tool calls, to show what appears in traces. If added:\n\n1. Add a test method in `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/tests/Samples/Sample_Responses_Tracing.cs` wrapping tool-call tracing setup with `#region Snippet:Sample_ResponsesToolCallTracing` / `#endregion`.\n2. Insert a ```` ```C# Snippet:Sample_ResponsesToolCallTracing` ```` placeholder in the README after the new \"Traced tool types\" table.\n\nThis is optional — the table in the README already describes what is traced without requiring a live code sample.\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README already contains all required sections:\n\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client)\n2. ✅ Key concepts\n3. ✅ Examples\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\n5. ✅ Next steps\n6. ✅ Contributing\n\nOnly the content within the \"Tracing\" subsection and the CHANGELOG need updating.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai` — only needed if a new snippet was added in Step 2\n4. `dotnet format sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\n","title":"[Azure AI Extensions OpenAI] Docs: Document expanded tool call tracing support in OpenTelemetry section"},"name":"create_issue"}}}
